name: Lateefah Simon
subject: My BART story is personal
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LATEEFAH FOR CONGRESS
body:
I want to tell you a little more about my time serving on the BART Board of Directors.
# [](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/go/39?t=1&akid=62%2E1163%2EJwWzNO)
Hey! It’s Lateefah! 👋🏾
I want to tell you a little bit more about my journey leading up to my current run for Congress in California’s 12th District, starting with my work on the BART Board of Directors. 🚆
[**But before I get into my role on the BART Board of Directors, if you’re short on time, or are already ready to invest in this movement and the work that I seek to bring to Congress, go ahead and chip in anything you can right now! We’ve set a monthly fundraising goal of $5,000 in grassroots donations.**](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/go/131?t=2&akid=62%2E1163%2EJwWzNO)
As a working single mom who doesn’t drive, _it’s no exaggeration to say that my life depends on a safe and sound public transportation system_. I understand firsthand how insufficient, expensive, and unreliable transit affects people with disabilities, low-income families, and middle class workers.
And in 2016, I realized that the best way I could help working families was to represent people like me on the BART Board. So, **I ran for BART Board —**** _and I won_**.
In this seat, I’ve championed progressive change — prioritizing affordable housing, law enforcement oversight, reduced fares for low income people and youth — and affected real progress across a district that spans part of California’s 12th Congressional District. I’m proud of the work that I did at BART where I fought to push the transit system in the direction of social justice and equity, and ensure that the system works for the people who need it most.
Now, I’m excited to keep building on my decades of proven leadership and my experience of bringing unlikely allies to the table to solve problems all the way to Congress.
[**Together, we’re building a powerful movement that will have ripple effects of critical change for our communities. Can you help support the work that I’m doing right now to organize and get out the vote across California’s 12th district?**](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/go/131?t=3&akid=62%2E1163%2EJwWzNO)
**[Donate $10 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em-20230717?amount=10&refcode=em20230717&refcode2=62_1163_JwWzNO&akid=62%2E1163%2EJwWzNO)**
Our community is so powerful when we stand shoulder-to-shoulder for things we believe in. **_I know together we can win this seat, strengthen our progressive representation in Congress, and win real change for our neighborhoods._**
**[Can you help me get there today? A donation of any amount will make a huge difference in strengthening our movement as we fight to reach our $5,000 grassroots July fundraising goal.](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/go/131?t=4&akid=62%2E1163%2EJwWzNO) **
Thank you,
Lateefah Simon
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em-wrapper?refcode=em20230717&t=5&refcode2=62_1163_JwWzNO&akid=62%2E1163%2EJwWzNO)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=6&akid=62%2E1163%2EJwWzNO)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=7&akid=62%2E1163%2EJwWzNO)

To give by check:
Lateefah for Congress
1714 Franklin St #100 - 438,
Oakland, CA, 94612
Email us: [info@lateefahsimon.com](mailto:info@lateefahsimon.com)
PAID FOR BY LATEEFAH FOR CONGRESS

name: Tennessee Democratic Party
subject: Good trouble
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66638560/417040034/1746233935?refcode=AU_TNDP_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20230717_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzEzNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMGVlMzM0OC1lNzI0LWVlMTEtYTliYi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ng6npsywRJGDYP9GrCA3Qzq6DSeOoJh1Gex-IcptGuc=)
---
**Peter — On this day in 2020, we lost a titan in the movement for a fairer and more just nation in Representative John Lewis.** Today, to honor his legacy, we’re calling on a renewed focus to expand voting rights across Tennessee and secure this essential right to participate in our democracy for marginalized and disenfranchised communities in every county. [**Can we count on you to add your name right now to support expanding voting rights across Tennessee and help us honor John Lewis’s legacy?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66638568/417040042/1497743539?refcode=AU_TNDP_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20230717_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzEzNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMGVlMzM0OC1lNzI0LWVlMTEtYTliYi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ng6npsywRJGDYP9GrCA3Qzq6DSeOoJh1Gex-IcptGuc=&emci=a4ee3348-e724-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&emdi=b0ee3348-e724-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&ceid=6362892) | [SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66638569/417040043/1497743539?refcode=AU_TNDP_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20230717_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzEzNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMGVlMzM0OC1lNzI0LWVlMTEtYTliYi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ng6npsywRJGDYP9GrCA3Qzq6DSeOoJh1Gex-IcptGuc=&emci=a4ee3348-e724-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&emdi=b0ee3348-e724-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&ceid=6362892)
---
John Lewis was a Fisk graduate and an integral student activist in the Civil Rights movement. As a member of the Nashville Student Movement, Rep. Lewis desegregated lunch counters in downtown Nashville and held non-violent workshops in order to train organizers in non-violent tactics to achieve racial progress.
He was one of the thirteen original Freedom Riders, who fought to integrate buses, facing violent mobs and police who worked to maintain the second-class status of Black Americans.
_via the Tennessean_
While Tennessee Republicans are working every day to undo it (even trying to rename John Lewis Way in Nashville, the street where the General Assembly sits, after Donald Trump,) John Lewis’s legacy reminds us that our freedom and our democracy are not just handed down from generation to generation. These fundamental pillars of American society must be fought for and won every generation and that agents of white supremacy will do anything to oppress marginalized communities.
**His legacy also reminds us that this fight is a righteous one but may require a little bit of “good trouble” in order to achieve progress.** Our entire team walks in the footsteps of organizers and activists like John Lewis, who spent his life working towards a better and more just life for everyone, but especially Black Americans.
[**Peter, join us in honoring the legacy of Representative John Lewis by reaffirming our commitment to the right to vote today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66638571/417040045/1497743539?refcode=AU_TNDP_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20230717_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzEzNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMGVlMzM0OC1lNzI0LWVlMTEtYTliYi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ng6npsywRJGDYP9GrCA3Qzq6DSeOoJh1Gex-IcptGuc=&emci=a4ee3348-e724-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&emdi=b0ee3348-e724-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&ceid=6362892)

clean_body: | = Peter — On this day in 2020, we lost a titan in the movement for a fairer and more just nation in Representative John Lewis. Today, to honor his legacy, we’re calling on a renewed focus to expand voting rights across Tennessee and secure this essential right to participate in our democracy for marginalized and disenfranchised communities in every county. | John Lewis was a Fisk graduate and an integral student activist in the Civil Rights movement. As a member of the Nashville Student Movement, Rep. Lewis desegregated lunch counters in downtown Nashville and held non-violent workshops in order to train organizers in non-violent tactics to achieve racial progress. He was one of the thirteen original Freedom Riders, who fought to integrate buses, facing violent mobs and police who worked to maintain the second-class status of Black Americans. via the Tennessean While Tennessee Republicans are working every day to undo it (even trying to rename John Lewis Way in Nashville, the street where the General Assembly sits, after Donald Trump,) John Lewis’s legacy reminds us that our freedom and our democracy are not just handed down from generation to generation. These fundamental pillars of American society must be fought for and won every generation and that agents of white supremacy will do anything to oppress marginalized communities. His legacy also reminds us that this fight is a righteous one but may require a little bit of “good trouble” in order to achieve progress. Our entire team walks in the footsteps of organizers and activists like John Lewis, who spent his life working towards a better and more just life for everyone, but especially Black Americans. — Tennessee Democratic Party Tennessee Democratic Party 319 Plus Park Blvd Nashville, TN 37217 United States | mailto:info@tndp.org | CAROL V. ABNEY, TREASURER NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. CONTRIBUTIONS OR GIFTS TO TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY ARE NOT TAX DEDUCTIBLE.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Matthew=20Barton?=
subject: No Bones About It
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/bartonfordesoto/no-bones-about-it?e=3c5d31b5ad)
---
| |
---
| Liberal prosecutors are attacking conservatives every chance they get. Just last week, Oregon’s liberal Attorney General, Ellen Rosenblum, defeated several conservative sheriffs in a case infringing on our Second Amendment rights.

Last spring, Rosenblum and 13 other liberal Attorney Generals joined Mexico — YES, MEXICO — in suing the American operations of gun manufacturers Smith & Wesson, Ruger, Colt, Glock, and others.

And before that, a Democrat New York D.A. indicted President Trump on a misdemeanor, turned Felony.

Even here in the Mid-South, District Attorney Steve Mulroy lets Memphis criminals go with a slap on the hand, just to go on and commit even more crimes.

What do these liberal Attorney Generals and District Attorneys have in common?

GEORGE SOROS.

SOROS has been attacking the Second Amendment for more than 30 years, and he continues his charge by funding the campaigns of liberal prosecutors and Congressmen throughout the country.

One of those is Congressman Cartwright, a Joe Biden endorsed candidate.

My opponent is running for D.A. as a Republican, but he is supporting radical Democrats like Cartwright, who attack our gun rights and support killing babies. What's worse, he even voted for the Democrat Presidential Candidate in 2020 and 2016. We have enough Democrat D.A.'s who are politically weaponizing government and attacking conservatives in America.

I’ll make no bones about it. We don’t need another. [Will you help me defeat my opponent who is masquerading around as a Republican? I know it's a lot to ask in this economy, but this race is important. Even $5 will help us get our message to voters.](https://bartonfordesoto.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0289922a9bceb4e2230228a3d&id=19c48591e8&e=3c5d31b5ad)

America does not need another NEVER-TRUMPER, Democrat D.A. pretending to be one of us.
---
| | | | [HELP MATTHEW BEAT GEORGE SOROS](https://bartonfordesoto.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0289922a9bceb4e2230228a3d&id=e520b9c858&e=3c5d31b5ad)
---

Thank you and God Bless you,
---
| | |
---
| Matthew Barton
_Sent from my iPhone_

PS: _If you believe you’ve received this email message in error - or do not wish to receive future updates from me or my team for any reason - [you can click here to unsubscribe](https://bartonfordesoto.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0289922a9bceb4e2230228a3d&id=2e1e292ced&e=3c5d31b5ad)[.](https://bartonfordesoto.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0289922a9bceb4e2230228a3d&id=c0d210111a&e=3c5d31b5ad)_
---
| |
---

**Meet Matthew Barton**
---

Matthew Barton grew up in Petal, Mississippi in a blue-collar family. His mother was a director at Tupperware and his father owned the small, family business – Barton Fence Company – where Matthew worked growing up.
After attending public school, Matthew attended Pearl River Community College and Warner University, where he studied Business Administration. He holds a law degree from Ole Miss Law, where he graduated Cum Laude and received the interviewing and counseling award.
In 2011, Matthew married his high school sweetheart, Megan. After graduating from Ole Miss Law in 2015, they moved to DeSoto County where Matthew started a law firm and Megan continued working at the Desoto County Schools as a Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant.
During law school, Matthew served as an intern in the DeSoto County District Attorney’s Office where he presented cases to grand jury, tried cases to verdict, and performed all functions of a practicing attorney. He has served as a prosecutor for the City of Southaven and the City of Horn Lake and now serves as a special prosecutor for Southaven, Horn Lake, and DeSoto County Justice Courts – where he’s established a strict, tough on crime reputation.
As an attorney, Mathew has dealt with every type of criminal matter ranging from DUI to capital murder, in more than 37 courts across Mississippi.
Matthew is a member of the Federalist Society, a conservative legal association, an enhanced conceal carry permit holder, Krewe of Hernando member, NRA member, and donates his time serving as a pro bono attorney to Veterans every year.
Today, Matthew, his wife Megan, and their daughter Eleanor live in Hernando where Matthew owns his private law firm. Eleanor attends Pre-K at Hernando United Methodist Church and Megan is a stay at home mom after seven years with DeSoto County schools.

Mondaire’s running to deliver meaningful change for the people of the Hudson Valley, because that’s where he’s from and that’s who he works for. Not the fossil fuel industry. Not pharmaceutical or insurance companies. Not big banks.

Mondaire’s opponent Mike Lawler—the former oil and gas lobbyist-turned-Congressman—represents all that is broken in Washington. He’ll say what you want to hear, but at the end of the day, he’s working for the corporations and Big Oil lobbyists who fund his campaign.

We know those special interests aren’t going down without a fight. That’s why everyday supporters are so critical to this race. When we have a steady stream of donations coming in, we’ll have the resources to go up against whatever Lawler and his MAGA Republican supporters spend on this raise.

[**It’s time to show Mike Lawler what happens when he ignores the people he was elected to serve. Pitch in $5—or whatever you can—to Mondaire’s campaign to help us beat Lawler, take back the House majority, and hit our first campaign mid-month fundraising goal.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66638658/417040535/13284604?refcode=em23_230717_MM2_fndd_gq_act___TM_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230717_MM2_fndd_gq_act___TM_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=mondaire-jones&refcode2=2a9d7a62-e824-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODAzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYTlkN2E2Mi1lODI0LWVlMTEtYTliYi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dMr2XuHJI4QgBXB1ios07DUss1EmcpfWKdLk9j-uLi8=)

Matt’s budget reform efforts continue to receive strong conservative praise.
* In the past week, Matt joined [Hugh Hewitt](http://links.dolanforsenate.com/u/click?_t=879ec3883fbb432d91bdf6fa5c9b36c6&_m=67e0a75cd46a4c37be18b087b68f0c81&_e=_QgdkLsvM0WgCFrb2aoryt-HbfqdS1d_FaliJ5vmYfP6_4sC-pt5kTK_JT2M_9CdUMmG__gawwNjJlwVGVmmOeKzAzVyflwp8_UbcAilb0bMXAIyXQO7ctGQO-Cc6iKX6cc-6jysRS0JjJIgbxqZfcg_1NtLHXYpywje9LTfIGhbAxrgwNqIiXUqAgu5HZKXntLuQM6fdQwW9f9RBaDvhDDUPherWmwbP03CwVV1egY9ZsigCF-jkoA3_xpI75PXOpk9V9mwEgBwMV6ofYehfDD3ifCslSMxpYs5FrKUlMndSt-o1mlR9qbLs26HXkqdVdbZLupaMdVnlvmXYjgQaw%3D%3D), [Brian Thomas](http://links.dolanforsenate.com/u/click?_t=879ec3883fbb432d91bdf6fa5c9b36c6&_m=67e0a75cd46a4c37be18b087b68f0c81&_e=_QgdkLsvM0WgCFrb2aorynUhZsK9DV0w9KOLcwm3o8lJwCVHFBN_afdTgCtM2tpbCWVu9A2F5ONY4_3-QG_Q3kG4-37O5k-cYL_aIsNCjNVkxyJ831YWObbHgck8yqUGiCHEhTj2VbLLU1WrPk9RhS1FRegJnN0hkTcFAW4vzoFbYalkp99axQY98MVlsl_PJ0N0YBjWUIj6P5jVZJ6_dlMQFZ7BLS4ncmfqLVj4Yf5OGswEVKAmvl1125hIpEDZ7ZueTrt1M32i9ThyoJOWPZVFti51BpSiqEueVkEC6tI%3D), Scott Sands, Brandon Boxer and other leading pundits across Ohio to discuss his bold conservative reform efforts with the state budget.
* The balanced budget produced in the State Senate includes more than $3 billion in tax relief for Ohio taxpayers, families and businesses. It expands school choice, funds police, reduces spending and even provides much-needed investments in mental health services.
* The budget has been praised by Americans for Tax Reform, Americans for Prosperity, the Buckeye Institute and conservative groups across Ohio.

clean_body: | | | July 17, 2023 Bringing you the latest news, updates and what you need to know about the race for U.S. Senate in Ohio Happy Monday! | Three Things to Know This Week: 1. Matt’s budget reform efforts continue to receive strong conservative praise. 2\. Matt continues to expand his already significant resource lead in the GOP Primary. 3\. Dolan door knocking is coming to your community! Get up to speed... | | | Matt’s budget reform efforts continue to receive strong conservative praise. In the past week, Matt joined , , Scott Sands, Brandon Boxer and other leading pundits across Ohio to discuss his bold conservative reform efforts with the state budget. The balanced budget produced in the State Senate includes more than $3 billion in tax relief for Ohio taxpayers, families and businesses. It expands school choice, funds police, reduces spending and even provides much-needed investments in mental health services. The budget has been praised by Americans for Tax Reform, Americans for Prosperity, the Buckeye Institute and conservative groups across Ohio. | | | | | | | Matt continues to expand his already significant resource lead in the GOP Primary and has been named the most competitive Republican challenger to Sherrod Brown! The Q2 fundraising numbers are in... and Matt Dolan significantly outpaced the GOP field in primary cash on hand! Matt has $3.8 million in fundraising and Bernie Moreno has $905,000 as of July 1. ECU’s polling institute conducted the first independent survey in the race for U.S. Senate in Ohio. It found Matt Dolan is not only ahead among announced Republican candidates for U.S. Senate, but he also is the most formidable challenger to Sherrod Brown! | | | | | Dolan door knocking is coming to your community! We’re joining county parties and grassroots supporters in conducting door knocking in support of Matt and also #VoteYESOhio every weekend and most week nights. If you’re interested in supporting Matt, please sign-up on our or email mailto:nick@dolanforohio.com?subject=Volunteer%20to%20help%20Matt%20Dolan!%20 (nick@dolanforOhio.com) and we’ll provide you with literature and a walk list for your community or county! Matt is the leading candidate for U.S. Senate, help us defeat Sherrod Brown! | | | | | | | | | | | | | Interested in joining Team Dolan? Matt Dolan needs your help to bring our conservative voices to the U.S. Senate, consider contributing below to help us WIN in Ohio! | | | Forward this email to a friend! Visit DolanforOhio.com for more information on Matt Dolan’s campaign. | | | | ---|---|---|--- | | This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Don't want to receive our emails anymore?

body:
secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-july2023?refcode=071723 [secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-july2023?refcode=071723]
Friend,
Have you heard of the Political Contribution Refund program? It's a program here in Minnesota that enables you to contribute $50 (or $100 per couple) to a participating campaign once per year and have it refunded to you by the state. That's right, you can make a contribution to support the great work our Minnesota House DFL is doing and get your money back!
Friend, can we count on you to help protect our House DFL Majority and make your refundable donation today?
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
Donate $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-july2023?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=071723]
Donate $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-july2023?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=071723]
Or, another amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hdfl-july2023?express_lane_other=true&refcode=071723]
If you’ve already made your PCR contribution to another campaign, please ignore this message.
We know MAGA Republicans are going to be working hard to flip the Minnesota House and try to undo all the good work we did this year: protecting abortion access, funding public education at historic levels, passing paid family medical leave, and so much more.
Thank you for your support of the House DFL, friend. Together we can protect our House DFL Majority and make Minnesota a state that works better for everyone.
House DFL

name: Sen. Heidi Campbell
subject: a Kesha tweet to make you smile on a Monday, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Campbell for Mayor
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230717_em_fr_b1_HC_30_90_10_AllD [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230717_em_fr_b1_HC_30_90_10_AllD]
Peter,
We know how hard it can be to stay positive when the Monday blues start to creep in.
The beginning of the work week can be tough, but that doesn’t mean we can’t smile and enjoy some positivity.
That’s why I wanted to share a heartwarming endorsement that made me smile over the past few days.
I want to tell you all about it, but if you don't have time to read the rest right now – Peter, can you take 15 seconds to donate to my campaign to put Nashville first today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230717_em_fr_b1_HC_30_90_10_AllD]
GIVE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230717_em_fr_b1_HC_30_90_10_AllD]
Peter, I am deeply honored and grateful to be endorsed for Nashville mayor by Kesha:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230717_em_fr_b1_HC_30_90_10_AllD [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230717_em_fr_b1_HC_30_90_10_AllD]
There is no artist out there with a heart as big and pure as Kesha, and I am so grateful and humbled to have her support.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230717_em_fr_b1_HC_30_90_10_AllD [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230717_em_fr_b1_HC_30_90_10_AllD]
Peter, will you join Kesha and the other amazing artists, community leaders, elected officials, and organizations supporting my campaign for Nashville mayor with a contribution today? (It would make me smile.) >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230717_em_fr_b1_HC_30_90_10_AllD]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230717_em_fr_b1_HC_30_90_10_AllD]
With immense gratitude,
Heidi
Heidi Campbell is a Nashville native, Mom, former music industry executive and the first female mayor of the city of Oak Hill.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230717_em_fr_b1_HC_30_90_10_AllD [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/campbell-email?refcode=20230717_em_fr_b1_HC_30_90_10_AllD]
As mayor, Heidi established a track record for fiscal discipline, balancing the budget every year of her Mayorship, eliminating my own salary, and returning $2M to my city’s reserves.
Heidi was elected in November of 2020 to represent District 20 in the Tennessee State Senate. Since then, she’s been an outspoken advocate for science, the LGBTQ community, public education, local control, women's and kids' issues, Medicaid expansion, and the environment.
Heidi sponsored or co-sponsored over 100 pieces of legislation in her first year in the senate, including legislation to allow cities to offer paid family leave, increase funding for school counselors and reading interventionists, and make it easier to cast a vote.
Heidi is focused on sustainable infrastructure, economic development and job creation, fully funding our public schools and supporting educators, expanding access to affordable healthcare, investing in suicide prevention, making certain that vulnerable children in state custody are not falling through the cracks, and ensuring that every Tennessean - regardless of ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age or ability - can achieve the American Dream.
Election Day is Thursday, August 3, 2023.

name: Nick Melvoin
subject: A national summer camp program would tackle these challenges head-on.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nick Melvoin for Congress - FEC ID #C00831289
body:
We need more than summer school. We need kids to be able to play, to be outdoors instead of behind screens.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66638864/417041511/2045957508?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQxNjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzA2MTY0MzktZTkyNC1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=oaTh_8ySys8ZiAgP3V1SQneXqYUnph-jmKyjLDPqVBo=&emci=7c4bdd57-c224-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&emdi=c0616439-e924-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&ceid=10481059)
---

| | Peter, Thanks to President Biden's American Families Plan, $1.8 trillion has been set aside to ensure that more families can access quality, affordable education and childcare. Parents and children have already witnessed the impact of this historic investment, and in the peak of the summer an opportunity comes to mind: **federally funded free summer day camp for all school-aged children.** There are several benefits to this proposal: **1\. Reduce the inequities that affect our children**. While more affluent children go off to summer camp, travel or participate in an array of activities, kids in low-income neighborhoods are _five times_ less likely to have that opportunity than their wealthier peers. **It would give kids a safe, enriching place to go while their parents work without incurring the crippling costs of childcare.** With more than 80% of families worrying about basic childcare needs — this would help millions of families. **2\. Combat summer academic learning loss.** Research shows that the average student will lose 17–34% of the prior school year's learning during summer break.

School districts nationwide have had to spend millions on summer school to mitigate the learning loss that took place during the height of the pandemic. But this issue recurs every summer at varying degrees.

**Summer camp would provide plenty of opportunities for further academic enrichment while also addressing something even more fundamental: socio-emotional healing and growth.** **3\. We can prevent millions of kids from slipping into isolation during the coming summer months.** Issues with mental health have taken a dramatic toll on our kids in the last few years, leading to increased cases of depression, anxiety, and suicidal thoughts.

The opportunity for emotional and interpersonal growth during the summer is tremendous.

Having worked with children both as a teacher and as a camp director, I've seen resilience taught just as effectively on ropes courses, athletic fields, lakes and in the mountains as in a classroom. **A national summer camp program would strengthen children's resilience, fortify their sense of self, and help to fill the gaping holes in our social safety net that the COVID-19 pandemic has both revealed and exacerbated**. 4\. **This will stimulate local economies.** By hiring young adults and college students on summer break to serve as counselors, the program would provide an economic boost to and give young adults a meaningful way to connect to their communities. **Implementing a national free summer camp is more affordable than it might sound.** We already have the land: public school campuses, which could be kept open during the summer with federal support — and national parks and other landmarks, which could offer free entrance for camp programs to visit. And for the thousands of camps that already exist, the federal government could help expand slots for low-income families. We need more than summer school. We need kids to be able to play, to be outdoors instead of behind screens. We need to help kids build new relationships, not just with each other, but with mentors and role models in the form of counselors. And we need kids to connect with, and come to love, the planet that we're asking them to help save. clean_body: We need more than summer school. We need kids to be able to play, to be outdoors instead of behind screens. | | | | Peter, Thanks to President Biden's American Families Plan, $1.8 trillion has been set aside to ensure that more families can access quality, affordable education and childcare. Parents and children have already witnessed the impact of this historic investment, and in the peak of the summer an opportunity comes to mind: federally funded free summer day camp for all school-aged children. There are several benefits to this proposal: 1\. Reduce the inequities that affect our children. While more affluent children go off to summer camp, travel or participate in an array of activities, kids in low-income neighborhoods are five times less likely to have that opportunity than their wealthier peers. It would give kids a safe, enriching place to go while their parents work without incurring the crippling costs of childcare. With more than 80% of families worrying about basic childcare needs — this would help millions of families. 2\. Combat summer academic learning loss. Research shows that the average student will lose 17–34% of the prior school year's learning during summer break. School districts nationwide have had to spend millions on summer school to mitigate the learning loss that took place during the height of the pandemic. But this issue recurs every summer at varying degrees. Summer camp would provide plenty of opportunities for further academic enrichment while also addressing something even more fundamental: socio-emotional healing and growth. 3\. We can prevent millions of kids from slipping into isolation during the coming summer months. Issues with mental health have taken a dramatic toll on our kids in the last few years, leading to increased cases of depression, anxiety, and suicidal thoughts. The opportunity for emotional and interpersonal growth during the summer is tremendous. Having worked with children both as a teacher and as a camp director, I've seen resilience taught just as effectively on ropes courses, athletic fields, lakes and in the mountains as in a classroom. A national summer camp program would strengthen children's resilience, fortify their sense of self, and help to fill the gaping holes in our social safety net that the COVID-19 pandemic has both revealed and exacerbated. 4\. This will stimulate local economies. By hiring young adults and college students on summer break to serve as counselors, the program would provide an economic boost to and give young adults a meaningful way to connect to their communities. Implementing a national free summer camp is more affordable than it might sound. We already have the land: public school campuses, which could be kept open during the summer with federal support — and national parks and other landmarks, which could offer free entrance for camp programs to visit. And for the thousands of camps that already exist, the federal government could help expand slots for low-income families. We need more than summer school. We need kids to be able to play, to be outdoors instead of behind screens. We need to help kids build new relationships, not just with each other, but with mentors and role models in the form of counselors. And we need kids to connect with, and come to love, the planet that we're asking them to help save. More soon, Nick Melvoin | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | = | = | = | = ---|---|---|--- | | | Paid for by Nick Melvoin for Congress - FEC ID #C00831289 |

Dear Friend,

Fourteen years ago, I founded an organization from my parent’s home with the intent of using undercover investigative journalism to expose the corrupt.

You’ve seen my work. Most Americans have.

In February, after releasing a historic investigation on Pfizer, the Board of Directors of the organization I created from scratch, ousted me.

It was a blow, but things happen for a reason. Today, I’m unleashed to do my work through my new organization, OMG, without any obstruction and **no one to stop me.**

Since I was fired, I’ve chosen not to look back. I want to move forward with my work, with happiness and spirit. **And I move forward carrying with me the many lessons I learned.**

Those 14 years were formative.

When I started, my hands would tremble when I confronted a target of my investigations. It took time, but I overcame my fears of confrontation by grounding myself in principle.

I learned about trust, loyalty, and leadership.

Most importantly, I learned the hard way the true depth of Michael Jordan’s quote on leadership: “Winning has a price. **And leadership has a price**.”

I’ve been sued for exposing the truth more times than I can count. And, get this, **yesterday, I was sued in federal court by the very organization I founded.**

In my opinion, their intent is to shut me down now that they no longer have any control over me.

And this lawsuit is going to cost me a fortune! That’s why I’m writing you today to **[ask for your support to my legal defense fund.](http://email.email.freethinkerdaily.com/c/eJxsz7GOnzAMBvCnMRsocUhy_4GhLaVTx7u1MomBFAIoBFW8fQU6der6-Sd_NsV9CcPVgP0KiFPwByCC-gKIZ1Yf9P7-M13l_L0uf-gPHO8hfgPEfO38T34uqThSWO7UtoVvaiQ5uIIbad5eBl9a22Jq3oR5WSHQSIu1Yell35MdUPfWD6hkERoUqISVFqVCpStRS8c1vQwZNsgaavEUVUNizlNYZ06ewnJVbovF0kw578dzWAfYHezOxBWt7Ld8C8BuCT2nfJXjSckDdr8p8lFuM_PAJeVMbi5jX24RVHfm-OvYzuQYVPsfCGhuEtmHM4Jq74IncRR3CuMKqo39Z5Y53WbIXgMaitu5ZlAt6iI1fv8TliUcxkItxudBt8W_AQAA___oBILQ)**

My friends with the non-profit, Liberty Guard, have set up **[the fund to pay attorney costs](http://email.email.freethinkerdaily.com/c/eJxsz7GOnzAMBvCnMRsocUhy_4GhLaVTx7u1MomBFAIoBFW8fQU6der6-Sd_NsV9CcPVgP0KiFPwByCC-gKIZ1Yf9P7-M13l_L0uf-gPHO8hfgPEfO38T34uqThSWO7UtoVvaiQ5uIIbad5eBl9a22Jq3oR5WSHQSIu1Yell35MdUPfWD6hkERoUqISVFqVCpStRS8c1vQwZNsgaavEUVUNizlNYZ06ewnJVbovF0kw578dzWAfYHezOxBWt7Ld8C8BuCT2nfJXjSckDdr8p8lFuM_PAJeVMbi5jX24RVHfm-OvYzuQYVPsfCGhuEtmHM4Jq74IncRR3CuMKqo39Z5Y53WbIXgMaitu5ZlAt6iI1fv8TliUcxkItxudBt8W_AQAA___oBILQ)** that will quickly pile up.

As I mentioned, this isn’t my first rodeo so I know that **the cost will exceed $500,000**.

Good attorneys aren’t cheap, and my pockets are not deep.

No one gets wealthy doing the work that I do. Trust me.

That’s where you come in. **[Will you chip in and help me defend myself](http://email.email.freethinkerdaily.com/c/eJxsz7GOnzAMBvCnMRsocUhy_4GhLaVTx7u1MomBFAIoBFW8fQU6der6-Sd_NsV9CcPVgP0KiFPwByCC-gKIZ1Yf9P7-M13l_L0uf-gPHO8hfgPEfO38T34uqThSWO7UtoVvaiQ5uIIbad5eBl9a22Jq3oR5WSHQSIu1Yell35MdUPfWD6hkERoUqISVFqVCpStRS8c1vQwZNsgaavEUVUNizlNYZ06ewnJVbovF0kw578dzWAfYHezOxBWt7Ld8C8BuCT2nfJXjSckDdr8p8lFuM_PAJeVMbi5jX24RVHfm-OvYzuQYVPsfCGhuEtmHM4Jq74IncRR3CuMKqo39Z5Y53WbIXgMaitu5ZlAt6iI1fv8TliUcxkItxudBt8W_AQAA___oBILQ)?**

When you do, I’ll be following up as the case progresses but I’ll also keep you informed of the great work I’m doing at OMG . . . and it’s exciting!

A fresh start, being forced to start over, I’ll admit is thrilling. And I’m starting armed with all of the lessons I’ve learned to avoid the pitfalls I’ve experienced.

Me and my teams are more efficient, we’re more productive, and our work hits like a sledgehammer.

But my work will be silenced if my former employer gets their way. Or the distractions of a court battle and its costs may become overwhelming.

Friend, **[I’m asking you to STAND UP FOR ME, so I can keep working](http://email.email.freethinkerdaily.com/c/eJxsz7GOnzAMBvCnMRsocUhy_4GhLaVTx7u1MomBFAIoBFW8fQU6der6-Sd_NsV9CcPVgP0KiFPwByCC-gKIZ1Yf9P7-M13l_L0uf-gPHO8hfgPEfO38T34uqThSWO7UtoVvaiQ5uIIbad5eBl9a22Jq3oR5WSHQSIu1Yell35MdUPfWD6hkERoUqISVFqVCpStRS8c1vQwZNsgaavEUVUNizlNYZ06ewnJVbovF0kw578dzWAfYHezOxBWt7Ld8C8BuCT2nfJXjSckDdr8p8lFuM_PAJeVMbi5jX24RVHfm-OvYzuQYVPsfCGhuEtmHM4Jq74IncRR3CuMKqo39Z5Y53WbIXgMaitu5ZlAt6iI1fv8TliUcxkItxudBt8W_AQAA___oBILQ)**. And I’ll commit to you that **I will NOT let you down**.

So please, **[make a donation to my legal defense fund through Liberty Guard](http://email.email.freethinkerdaily.com/c/eJxsz7GOnzAMBvCnMRsocUhy_4GhLaVTx7u1MomBFAIoBFW8fQU6der6-Sd_NsV9CcPVgP0KiFPwByCC-gKIZ1Yf9P7-M13l_L0uf-gPHO8hfgPEfO38T34uqThSWO7UtoVvaiQ5uIIbad5eBl9a22Jq3oR5WSHQSIu1Yell35MdUPfWD6hkERoUqISVFqVCpStRS8c1vQwZNsgaavEUVUNizlNYZ06ewnJVbovF0kw578dzWAfYHezOxBWt7Ld8C8BuCT2nfJXjSckDdr8p8lFuM_PAJeVMbi5jX24RVHfm-OvYzuQYVPsfCGhuEtmHM4Jq74IncRR3CuMKqo39Z5Y53WbIXgMaitu5ZlAt6iI1fv8TliUcxkItxudBt8W_AQAA___oBILQ)**. We have a long way to do to hit our goal, so please, donate as much as you can.

Friend, thank you for your direct or indirect support over the years. Your views, donations or simply talking about my work to friends and family has kept me going for 14 years . . . **and we cannot stop now!**

Respectfully,

James O’Keefe

P.S. I have 20 days to file my response to the lawsuit. I have to raise enough money to pay for a retainer and cover the legal work to defend against this lawsuit. clean_body: 14 years ago, I founded an organization from my parent’s home with the intent of using undercover investigative journalism to expose the corrupt. 14 years ago, I founded an organization from my parent’s home with the intent of using undercover investigative journalism to expose the corrupt. | | | | | | Dear Friend, Fourteen years ago, I founded an organization from my parent’s home with the intent of using undercover investigative journalism to expose the corrupt. You’ve seen my work. Most Americans have. In February, after releasing a historic investigation on Pfizer, the Board of Directors of the organization I created from scratch, ousted me. It was a blow, but things happen for a reason. Today, I’m unleashed to do my work through my new organization, OMG, without any obstruction and no one to stop me. Since I was fired, I’ve chosen not to look back. I want to move forward with my work, with happiness and spirit. And I move forward carrying with me the many lessons I learned. Those 14 years were formative. When I started, my hands would tremble when I confronted a target of my investigations. It took time, but I overcame my fears of confrontation by grounding myself in principle. I learned about trust, loyalty, and leadership. Most importantly, I learned the hard way the true depth of Michael Jordan’s quote on leadership: “Winning has a price. And leadership has a price.” I’ve been sued for exposing the truth more times than I can count. And, get this, yesterday, I was sued in federal court by the very organization I founded. In my opinion, their intent is to shut me down now that they no longer have any control over me. And this lawsuit is going to cost me a fortune! That’s why I’m writing you today to My friends with the non-profit, Liberty Guard, have set up that will quickly pile up. As I mentioned, this isn’t my first rodeo so I know that the cost will exceed $500,000. Good attorneys aren’t cheap, and my pockets are not deep. No one gets wealthy doing the work that I do. Trust me. That’s where you come in. ? When you do, I’ll be following up as the case progresses but I’ll also keep you informed of the great work I’m doing at OMG . . . and it’s exciting! A fresh start, being forced to start over, I’ll admit is thrilling. And I’m starting armed with all of the lessons I’ve learned to avoid the pitfalls I’ve experienced. Me and my teams are more efficient, we’re more productive, and our work hits like a sledgehammer. But my work will be silenced if my former employer gets their way. Or the distractions of a court battle and its costs may become overwhelming. Friend, . And I’ll commit to you that I will NOT let you down. So please, . We have a long way to do to hit our goal, so please, donate as much as you can. Friend, thank you for your direct or indirect support over the years. Your views, donations or simply talking about my work to friends and family has kept me going for 14 years . . . and we cannot stop now! Respectfully, James O’Keefe P.S. I have 20 days to file my response to the lawsuit. I have to raise enough money to pay for a retainer and cover the legal work to defend against this lawsuit. This is unfortunately urgent so please, .

Peter, as we kick off the summer months, I wanted to reach out personally to give you a campaign update.

It hasn’t even been seven full months since the new year began, but our race here in OH-13 is already in full swing. Over the past few months, multiple, non-partisan election forecasters have called our district a pure toss-up and a must-win seat for reclaiming the Democratic House Majority.

And on top of that, numerous Republican groups have already launched attack ad campaigns against us.

It couldn’t be more clear that this race is shaping up to be one of the most competitive in the country, once again.

**But, Peter, I believe in this campaign. I believe in the movement we are continuing to build here in Northeast Ohio. And I believe in our ability to continue to deliver real, lasting change for our community and our country.**

However, in a race this competitive, the only way we’re going to be successful is if we come together – _and stick together_ – as a grassroots team.

body:
https://www.engelforarizona.com/ [https://www.engelforarizona.com/]
If you stepped outside your door in the last couple of weeks, you might've noticed something: the intense, never-ending heat that is hitting Arizona, and much of the rest of the country.
As Arizonans, we're used to hot summer days. But high temperatures like this can be deadly.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel2024_foundingdonor?refcode=X_071723_Heat_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel2024_foundingdonor?refcode=X_071723_Heat_act]
As climate change worsens, extreme weather patterns are going to become the new normal. We need leaders in Congress who will help us prepare for, protect, and prevent these disasters.
Kirsten is that leader. She spent 30 years as an environmental lawyer, taking on polluters, studying Arizona's water crisis, and advocating for our families.
She'll fight to address the climate crisis without leaving anyone behind. With her leadership, we can build a sustainable future that lifts all boats — and keeps us safe when temperatures are shooting past 110 degrees.
Kirsten is the right choice for AZ-06. Will you chip in now to make sure she can flip this seat blue, help us take back control of the House, and fight for us in Congress? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel2024_foundingdonor?refcode=X_071723_Heat_act]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel2024_foundingdonor?refcode=X_071723_Heat_act]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel2024_foundingdonor?refcode=X_071723_Heat_act]
Thank you so much!
— Team Engel
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel2024_foundingdonor?refcode=X_071723_Heat_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel2024_foundingdonor?refcode=X_071723_Heat_act] Kirsten is a mom, an environmental attorney, and a former State Legislator who has spent the past six years fighting to restore funding to public schools, protect Arizona's natural resources, and make our communities safer.
She’s ready to take that fight to Washington to find common-sense solutions to the challenges we’re facing here in Arizona's Sixth Congressional District.
In 2022, Kirsten lost her race by one of the smallest margins in the country. Will you make sure she can build a winning campaign early by chipping in now? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel2024_foundingdonor?refcode=X_071723_Heat_act]

With her leadership, we can build a sustainable future that lifts all boats — and keeps us safe when temperatures are shooting past 110 degrees. Kirsten is the right choice for AZ-06. Will you chip in now to make sure she can flip this seat blue, help us take back control of the House, and fight for us in Congress? [] DONATE NOW [] DONATE NOW [] Thank you so much! — Team Engel [] Kirsten is a mom, an environmental attorney, and a former State Legislator who has spent the past six years fighting to restore funding to public schools, protect Arizona's natural resources, and make our communities safer. She’s ready to take that fight to Washington to find common-sense solutions to the challenges we’re facing here in Arizona's Sixth Congressional District. In 2022, Kirsten lost her race by one of the smallest margins in the country. Will you make sure she can build a winning campaign early by chipping in now? Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in.

name: Dr. Shawn Wilson for Governor
subject: Putting in the legwork
body:
image of ... [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/swlogo2.png]
TEAM WILSON WEEKLY UPDATES
Peter,
While my opponents avoid debates and focus on culture wars with a limited scope in our state, I’m rolling up my sleeves and talking through the issues with residents, like I did during my appearance on the UrbanCast [https://fb.watch/lN4TBMh9n-/] .
We are now just THREE MONTHS away from the primary election, and even less time separates us from the start of Early Voting. Let’s keep the momentum of the early campaign going and get the message out about the candidate committed to working for a better Louisiana for all people. It’s our time, Louisiana.
CHIP IN [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sw-web]
HIGHLIGHTS FROM THE CAMPAIGN TRAIL
Governor’s Forum at Southern University
Thank you to all 6 religious denominations and associations who joined forces to put on the faith-based gubernatorial forum hosted at Southern University !
I was grateful to share how I will address Louisianans' concerns and move our state forward. I also thank the four other candidates who attended.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Cn7Vh_e-2p8&t=6 [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Cn7Vh_e-2p8&t=6]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/Shawn%20Wilson%20%20Socia%20copy%20copy%20copy%20copy%20copy%20copy%20copy-High-Quality%20%287%29.jpg]
Monroe
Voting and access to the polls forms the bedrock principles of a functioning democracy. At the Ouachita Chapter of NAACP banquet in Monroe, I had the chance to discuss the importance of voting rights as their keynote speaker.
After my address, I connected with so many people who believe in protecting our right to vote, including the Mayor of Richwood, Gerald Brown .
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/My%20project-1%20-%202023-07-13T153357.225.png]
Jefferson Parish
The word is out!
Here is a Jefferson Parish resident who retired from a state job after 18 years of loyal service. She knows #TeamWilson will always stand with state employees... past, present, & future.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/IMG_20230710_125056224_processed.jpg]
Sheriff and Wardens forum
The Sheriffs and Wardens forum was very productive. I had a chance to share my support for supplemental pay, prevention, and local government.
Autonomy, accountability, & access are fundamental to my governing style with this group and the larger law enforcement community.
We have effectively partnered for more than 7 years, and our future will be built on that foundation.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/My%20project-1%20-%202023-07-14T100904.642.png]
Alexandria
I was grateful for the opportunity to visit the Grambling State University CENLA Alumni Chapter in Alexandria as they hosted “Gram Fam” for students and incoming freshmen from the region.
They provided a meal and care packages. I shared my history of investing in HBCUs and my commitment to keeping higher education affordable.
It was great to see Alums staying connected, especially the Gamma Gamma Chapter of Omega Psi Phi Fraternity , my brother’s initiation chapter.
egends born right here in Louisiana and the hope we have for our future!
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/My%20project-1%20-%202023-07-14T101215.520.png]
GIVING PRAISE
Pineville
Rocki and I joined the LA District United Pentecostal Church at their annual family night. The night was energized by lots of wonderful people and familiar faces. Alongside this night of community, we received an inspiring message at their worship service.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/My%20project-1%20-%202023-07-13T152204.497.png]
West Monroe/Monroe
Often times, church environments facilitate lasting connections and deep communal roots. Last weekend, Rocki and I made our first stop at Calvary Baptist Church , situated on a beautiful site in West Monroe, LA.
During our next stop at Piney Grove Missionary Baptist Church , I was reminded by Rev. Lorenzo Price that the first time we met was in a church setting. I was happy to spark our reconnection in a similar setting.
At our next stop, the congregation of Greater New Antioch Baptist Church welcomed us with open arms. There we received a prudent message titled “It Pays To Do What Is Right.” Just like this message, I believe in standing up for those around me and keeping the interest of the greater public close to my servant heart.
Faith based organizations occupy important spaces throughout Louisiana’s community. To effectively build lasting educational opportunities for our children, we must partner with these organizations as avenues for community outreach and engagement.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/My%20project-1%20-%202023-07-13T153904.966.png]
New Iberia
I spent an evening in New Iberia with the Union Sixth District Missionary Baptist Association for their annual session. It was a wonderful time spent with pastors and church members and a sermon by my friend and former church member Rev. Mark Lewis , now with Lilly Grove Missionary Baptist Church of Houston .
I was grateful to speak with and receive the support of New Iberia Councilmembers Marlon Lewis and Deedy Johnson-Reid and the personal endorsement of Rev. Carl F. Lewis .
Additionally, I was thankful for the informative talk with Patterson Councilmember R DeMale Bowden Jr. , Eugene Sonnier and Cassandra Butler , who all work as morticians or funeral directors and are concerned about both their business and our state's future.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/My%20project-1%20-%202023-07-14T101546.839.png]
We have the best team! #TeamWilson helped get the word out about my policy stances with fans and literature while I spoke at last weekend’s faith-based governor's forum.
They're working to help us realize Louisiana's bright future. Want to join the team? Sign up at wilsonforla.com/take-action.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/My%20project-1%20-%202023-07-13T152941.300.png]
wilsonforla.com/take-action [wilsonforla.com/take-action]
ENDORSEMENTS
Mansfield Mayor Thomas Jones
"Dr. Shawn Wilson is committed to finding new solutions to old problems to address crime and improve public safety," said Mansfield Mayor Thomas Jones. "It's time we elect a man with a vision like Dr. Wilson to lead our state to the next level. Let's get him elected."
https://www.wilsonforla.com/sen-cleo-fields-endorses-shawn-wilson-for-governor [https://www.wilsonforla.com/sen-cleo-fields-endorses-shawn-wilson-for-governor]
Sunset Mayor Charles James
"When I announced my bid for re-election, I noted that with planning and foresight, Sunset can position itself to take advantage of the opportunities for growth and development while still maintaining its special brand of community," said Sunset Mayor Charles James. "If we elect Dr. Shawn Wilson as Governor, I believe Louisiana can do the same. Dr. Wilson has the experience, ability, and commitment to grow our state. I wholeheartedly endorse his candidacy."
https://www.wilsonforla.com/sen-cleo-fields-endorses-shawn-wilson-for-governor [https://www.wilsonforla.com/sen-cleo-fields-endorses-shawn-wilson-for-governor]
New Orleans City Councilmember Joseph I. Giarrusso, III
"In order to jumpstart Louisiana's economic engine, we need a Governor with a history of securing federal funding and wisely using our state funds for the betterment of all Louisianans," said New Orleans City Councilmember Joe Giarrusso. "His willingness to engage with the energy of the future to create new, good paying jobs can make Louisiana again the supply chain leader for our country."
https://www.wilsonforla.com/sen-cleo-fields-endorses-shawn-wilson-for-governor [https://www.wilsonforla.com/sen-cleo-fields-endorses-shawn-wilson-for-governor]
New Orleans City Councilmember Eugene J. Green, Jr.
"It is my pleasure and honor to endorse not just a native son of New Orleans, but one whose accomplishments, character, and demeanor are exactly what Louisiana needs in a Governor," said New Orleans City Councilmember Eugene Green. "Dr. Shawn Wilson is a leader who truly listens to the people, one who truly understands how to facilitate deals and agreements that will benefit Louisianans. With Shawn Wilson as Governor of Louisiana, families can expect a commitment to improved outcomes in education, public safety, healthcare, and more. I proudly endorse Shawn Wilson for Governor."
https://www.wilsonforla.com/sen-cleo-fields-endorses-shawn-wilson-for-governor [https://www.wilsonforla.com/sen-cleo-fields-endorses-shawn-wilson-for-governor]
We've had so many successes over the last six-plus years, culminating in a $2 billion dollar deficit becoming a $2 billion dollar surplus.
If you want more positive change, join me in working for a better Louisiana for all of us.
It's our time, Louisiana.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mF9bRFntSvM [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mF9bRFntSvM]
To learn more about my candidacy visit wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com] .
With hope,
Shawn Wilson

[] TEAM WILSON WEEKLY UPDATES Peter, While my opponents avoid debates and focus on culture wars with a limited scope in our state, I’m rolling up my sleeves and talking through the issues with residents, like I did during my appearance on the UrbanCast [] . We are now just THREE MONTHS away from the primary election, and even less time separates us from the start of Early Voting. Let’s keep the momentum of the early campaign going and get the message out about the candidate committed to working for a better Louisiana for all people. It’s our time, Louisiana. CHIP IN [] HIGHLIGHTS FROM THE CAMPAIGN TRAIL Governor’s Forum at Southern University Thank you to all 6 religious denominations and associations who joined forces to put on the faith-based gubernatorial forum hosted at Southern University ! I was grateful to share how I will address Louisianans' concerns and move our state forward. I also thank the four other candidates who attended. [] [] Monroe Voting and access to the polls forms the bedrock principles of a functioning democracy. At the Ouachita Chapter of NAACP banquet in Monroe, I had the chance to discuss the importance of voting rights as their keynote speaker. After my address, I connected with so many people who believe in protecting our right to vote, including the Mayor of Richwood, Gerald Brown . [] Jefferson Parish The word is out! Here is a Jefferson Parish resident who retired from a state job after 18 years of loyal service. She knows #TeamWilson will always stand with state employees... past, present, & future. [] Sheriff and Wardens forum The Sheriffs and Wardens forum was very productive. I had a chance to share my support for supplemental pay, prevention, and local government. Autonomy, accountability, & access are fundamental to my governing style with this group and the larger law enforcement community. We have effectively partnered for more than 7 years, and our future will be built on that foundation. [] Alexandria I was grateful for the opportunity to visit the Grambling State University CENLA Alumni Chapter in Alexandria as they hosted “Gram Fam” for students and incoming freshmen from the region. They provided a meal and care packages. I shared my history of investing in HBCUs and my commitment to keeping higher education affordable. It was great to see Alums staying connected, especially the Gamma Gamma Chapter of Omega Psi Phi Fraternity , my brother’s initiation chapter. egends born right here in Louisiana and the hope we have for our future! Friend,
Tucker Carlson made a big prediction - and I completely agree with it!
_**" I think we're going to see Trump's emergence as the most significant thing that happened in American politics in 100 years, because he reoriented the Republican Party against the wishes of Republican leaders."**_
But I'm humble enough to know that the TRUE significance of our movement is YOU.
The reason our movement has such historic significance is because it took power away from the corrupt ruling class and returned it to **YOU** , the American people.
As I said at my Inauguration,_ "What truly matters is not which party controls our government, but whether our government is controlled by the people."_
**And that 's exactly why the disloyal Republicans known as RINOs are pledging to spend $370 MILLION to try and sabotage us in the GOP primary.**
These RINOs hate the idea of YOU being in control of your own government so much that they'd rather see Joe Biden win in 2024 than watch me return to the White House and put America First again.
But in 2024, we're going to prove Tucker Carlson right and ensure that the MAGA movement goes down in history books as the most significant thing in American politics in 100 YEARS.

body:
and let's arm the world while we are at it No images? [Click here](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-e-aaojl-trhiujldw-v/) [](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-r/)
## **Happy Monday! What better way to start your week than checking in with your friends on CaucusRoom!**
[](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-y/) **Grow your organization and keep the momentum going with our powerful platform.** [Schedule a CaucusRoom Live demo today!](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-j/) **Arming the world** [General Milley is sounding the alarm over the need to deliver weapons to Taiwan in the event of a Chinese invasion](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-t/). At this point who **aren't **we sending weapons to?! **The weakening dollar**
# President Biden, and his team of advisors have been systemically [weakening the standing of the U.S. dollar](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-i/) as the main reserve currency around the world. This should worry us all.
**The declining mental state of our President** Ben Carson is warning everyone to the [dangers of of Biden's mental state](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-d/), and as a real physician, he is an expert on the subject. **Reducing the global population** Our always eloquent Vice President recently said that to [reduce "climate change" we must "reduce population"](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-h/). Is she just saying the scary things out loud, or was this simply a HUGE gaffe?

**The weapons of war criminals** President Biden recently announced that the U.S. would be sending cluster munitions to Ukraine in their ongoing war with Russia. [Putin has vowed reciprocate in-kind if Ukraine uses the weapons](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-k/). So, who blinks first? **Let****'s [have a good laugh](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-u/)!**
* [**Duane got a new weapon, but now he has a new problem...**](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-o/)
* [**Scooby-doo and his friends surely can solve the White House cocaine mystery.**](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-x/)
* [**Emil has posted a great photo of their congressional delegation!**](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-m/)
* [**Ralph poses a really great question...**](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aaojl-trhiujldw-c/)

## Why Chris Christie:

Chris Christie is a proven leader who fearlessly tells it like it is. As a successful governor in a blue state with a track record of real accomplishments, Christie has consistently confronted challenging issues head-on, making difficult decisions without hesitation. To ensure a conservative Republican victory in the 2024 presidential election, we deserve an open, honest debate that focuses on critical issues. Join us in supporting Chris Christie's candidacy for president and ensuring his direct, no-nonsense agenda at the RNC-sponsored presidential debates. We wanted to reach out ONE LAST TIME and invite you to renew your 2023 sustaining membership. After today, you will be REMOVED from our membership roster. You 've fought with us for three consecutive years, and we need you to stick by our side as we go into battle this year. | Renewing your membership is of critical importance to the future of the Republican party. Here 's why renewing your membership for 2023 is so important: 1. You will be supporting conservatives across the country as they go on offense and bring the fight to the Radical Democrats. 2. You will be supporting our efforts to hold Joe Biden and the Radical Left accountable for their corruption and failures. 3. You will be an essential part of defending our majority and fighting for conservative values. "We can’t do it without you. Renew your membership before it’s too late" | | | Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Kevin McCarthy
subject: I’d be really grateful if you took the time to read this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McCarthy for Congress
body:
I'm hoping you'll read this all the way through.

| |
---
| | | This is Speaker of the House Kevin McCarthy. I wanted to take a few minutes today to share something personal with you, but it's going to take a bit of explaining. **If you get to the end and still don 't understand my commitment to America, then I'm just appreciative that you took the time to read this in its entirety.**

I wanted to take some time to discuss my background so you understand where I'm coming from when I'm fighting for the American people.
| | [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=3547b49db6da48ea9e8c52664a78cbf9&_e=IWMAPeukVvnkxAzws5WGbJ2ExxqHRsMPGad9ExGQrglVWNV_53cTWPsNKHFpTUfHzZSoCgglM8jzlQ9YcEnuk0UuasK_Y9_1wh1n7eYzY44j0m1I9e-nAYPGDOsTnub1N9DNU8SG1izteQ0IrkFt4KSTztFOS5gl5HC5fRn654nJJTa4UV254EuYlS4yDWsoaHkIARFqU6VA4xMkr1eJdQ3uIa-VhLr9ALr23v3vplFa7mjHyKky7nnxMLMBpMOLihhs2LBx8P6E-5Yl86ThD1yvpA3ZdcapbT3JVdDvGU_QD7SudLkxl0gqaFL9AuLlPPdfl4sp0WfYOWNK_VU3gRYPI7P0vlzRNhsUPsZEjJY2ChezXieWE2igtGdE7JpmL5_CWyZ1xmvohijqdlCX8L0wqcPHyFmQG7zhPhUNgcp9tIEsn-BuV358pgihv3pP "Kevin as a baby with his dad")
---
| [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=3547b49db6da48ea9e8c52664a78cbf9&_e=IWMAPeukVvnkxAzws5WGbJ2ExxqHRsMPGad9ExGQrglVWNV_53cTWPsNKHFpTUfHzZSoCgglM8jzlQ9YcEnuk0UuasK_Y9_1wh1n7eYzY44j0m1I9e-nAYPGDOsTnub1N9DNU8SG1izteQ0IrkFt4KSTztFOS5gl5HC5fRn654nJJTa4UV254EuYlS4yDWsoaHkIARFqU6VA4xMkr1eJdQ3uIa-VhLr9ALr23v3vplFa7mjHyKky7nnxMLMBpMOLihhs2LBx8P6E-5Yl86ThD1yvpA3ZdcapbT3JVdDvGU9V2ztgWsUKefH5im7FzoXV8etV6VOa_36kjkK2Y0L6pqmeP5Tw-PdiGKiWIiK_NBPuxySq3_y5kGfaPSLzxeTAp_LOIaDqaGqV6fqSSRQOseX94YYWPbnGI1QHgvwDvFrSbEoGWfeFtdpao4ZDfYen "Kevin with his siblings in 1968")
---
_My dad and I, circa 1966._ | _My siblings, Shelly and Mark, and I, circa 1968._

I come from a working-class background. My grandparents were immigrants. My mom, Roberta, was a homemaker. My dad, Owen, was an assistant city fire chief. **They instilled a strong work ethic in me that I carry with me every day.**
| [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=3547b49db6da48ea9e8c52664a78cbf9&_e=IWMAPeukVvnkxAzws5WGbJ2ExxqHRsMPGad9ExGQrglVWNV_53cTWPsNKHFpTUfHzZSoCgglM8jzlQ9YcEnuk0UuasK_Y9_1wh1n7eYzY44j0m1I9e-nAYPGDOsTnub1N9DNU8SG1izteQ0IrkFt4KSTztFOS5gl5HC5fRn654nJJTa4UV254EuYlS4yDWsoaHkIARFqU6VA4xMkr1eJdQ3uIa-VhLr9ALr23v3vplFa7mjHyKky7nnxMLMBpMOLihhs2LBx8P6E-5Yl86ThD1yvpA3ZdcapbT3JVdDvGU_iEoPUpigXP8QqoKEaEJ1YhafjCk5nj8LKWg0OG5RqnmeszSppySjwxA7afVAtsgYtEHBUoRhhVeTfdj6dHSaLt9-LuSISyQk0-aUVbDYZGDl4xxA1YL3HsJkeLtMAjlHp8lPOtKTWAj2clUkAeNPt "Kevin working the counter at Kevin O's Deli & McCarthy's Yogurt.")
---
_Working the counter at Kevin O's Deli & McCarthy's Yogurt. (Sorry for the image quality, but at least you'll know it's authentic!)_

When I was 19, I ran my first business, Kevin O's Deli, selling sandwiches out of the back of my uncle's yogurt shop. Eventually, I sold the deli to pay my way through college. It taught me a lot about personal responsibility, and **I quickly learned how overregulation by the government affects small businesses. **
| [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=3547b49db6da48ea9e8c52664a78cbf9&_e=IWMAPeukVvnkxAzws5WGbJ2ExxqHRsMPGad9ExGQrglVWNV_53cTWPsNKHFpTUfHzZSoCgglM8jzlQ9YcEnuk0UuasK_Y9_1wh1n7eYzY44j0m1I9e-nAYPGDOsTnub1N9DNU8SG1izteQ0IrkFt4KSTztFOS5gl5HC5fRn654nJJTa4UV254EuYlS4yDWsoaHkIARFqU6VA4xMkr1eJdQ3uIa-VhLr9ALr23v3vplFa7mjHyKky7nnxMLMBpMOLihhs2LBx8P6E-5Yl86ThD1yvpA3ZdcapbT3JVdDvGU-5E5MqY-xU1ustkReCzm3jpWiQaZpndDGpvjJbmH98TAVsniNh3RqPzr8Aj23JgrP4G8zSKWQHH431ujFihUiYV4_wmKOXyL8PaozBbX5ZJ4ef81AVd4r2C5AXoIqypMSOzppgqH4oXS3L7eC0Cz3f "Kevin speaking with a farmer on the campaign trail in 2010")
---
_Speaking with a farmer on the campaign trail in 2010._

After Kevin O's Deli and my experience with government overreach, I decided it was time to make a change. I knew public service would be a positive way for me to channel my frustration as a former small business owner, while also helping others in similar situations. Little did I know that working in the California State Assembly would pave the way to holding one of the most prestigious roles in our country: _**Speaker of the House of Representatives.**_
| [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=3547b49db6da48ea9e8c52664a78cbf9&_e=IWMAPeukVvnkxAzws5WGbJ2ExxqHRsMPGad9ExGQrglVWNV_53cTWPsNKHFpTUfHzZSoCgglM8jzlQ9YcEnuk0UuasK_Y9_1wh1n7eYzY44j0m1I9e-nAYPGDOsTnub1N9DNU8SG1izteQ0IrkFt4KSTztFOS5gl5HC5fRn654nJJTa4UV254EuYlS4yDWsoaHkIARFqU6VA4xMkr1eJdQ3uIa-VhLr9ALr23v3vplFa7mjHyKky7nnxMLMBpMOLihhs2LBx8P6E-5Yl86ThD1yvpA3ZdcapbT3JVdDvGU8O2nHNF0cjDZ279Oy4fhgDqrK2GIaAWSFy0xI6aGZxAu-o5Q2ohKJ7ZIi3_lfhKBMtceb9xBw6EuX7U2N_cwVaKolt-ox9y07_QSvFAQvZ8vPz5eWuPKS4m-bP8sEbtiCiExTdRFOICr32Ye-E7Iyi "Celebrating Republicans’ success in taking back the House in 2022.")
---
_Celebrating Republicans ' success in taking back the House in 2022_

This brings me to my main point: **my Commitment to America isn 't some political ploy - _it 's deeply personal._**

**As Speaker of the House, I promise to deliver on our commitment to America and the American people.

clean_body: I'm hoping you'll read this all the way through. | | | | | This is Speaker of the House Kevin McCarthy. I wanted to take a few minutes today to share something personal with you, but it's going to take a bit of explaining. If you get to the end and still don 't understand my commitment to America, then I'm just appreciative that you took the time to read this in its entirety. I wanted to take some time to discuss my background so you understand where I'm coming from when I'm fighting for the American people. | | "Kevin as a baby with his dad" | "Kevin with his siblings in 1968" My dad and I, circa 1966. | My siblings, Shelly and Mark, and I, circa 1968. I come from a working-class background. My grandparents were immigrants. My mom, Roberta, was a homemaker. My dad, Owen, was an assistant city fire chief. They instilled a strong work ethic in me that I carry with me every day. | "Kevin working the counter at Kevin O's Deli & McCarthy's Yogurt." Working the counter at Kevin O's Deli & McCarthy's Yogurt. (Sorry for the image quality, but at least you'll know it's authentic!) When I was 19, I ran my first business, Kevin O's Deli, selling sandwiches out of the back of my uncle's yogurt shop. Eventually, I sold the deli to pay my way through college. It taught me a lot about personal responsibility, and I quickly learned how overregulation by the government affects small businesses. | "Kevin speaking with a farmer on the campaign trail in 2010" Speaking with a farmer on the campaign trail in 2010. After Kevin O's Deli and my experience with government overreach, I decided it was time to make a change. I knew public service would be a positive way for me to channel my frustration as a former small business owner, while also helping others in similar situations. Little did I know that working in the California State Assembly would pave the way to holding one of the most prestigious roles in our country: Speaker of the House of Representatives. | "Celebrating Republicans’ success in taking back the House in 2022." Celebrating Republicans ' success in taking back the House in 2022 This brings me to my main point: my Commitment to America isn 't some political ploy - it 's deeply personal. As Speaker of the House, I promise to deliver on our commitment to America and the American people. "If you support my efforts as Speaker, please chip in whatever you can afford – $5, $10, even $25 if you can – to help me continue this fight." | | "STAND WITH SPEAKER MCCARTHY" If you've read this through to the end, thank you so much for your time. I hope you understand why our commitment to America is so important. I'm so grateful to have your support. Thank you, | "KOM Signature" Speaker of the House Kevin McCarthy | | "DONATE NOW" | | Paid for by McCarthy for Congress PO Box 12667 Bakersfield, CA 93389 Freedom Nation Today 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314 |

Peter,

**Things aren’t going as swimmingly as President Biden wants you to believe.** We’re $32 trillion in debt, Russia started a war in Europe, and teachers’ unions don’t want parents to have any say whatsoever about what our children are taught in school. Since President Biden was elected in 2020, the United States has lost energy independce, inflation hit an all time high, and cartels have taken over our southern border. Even with all of this happening, _**Joe Biden continues to do nothing.**_

I’m not painting a rosy picture because, Peter, the last three years have been a disaster.

**We need to get our country back on track.**

We can start by winning back the majority in the United States Senate. Doing so won’t be easy, **but on June 27th, when former Navy SEAL Tim Sheehy announced his candidacy, taking back the Senate became possible.** He’s running to beat progressive Montana Democrat, Jon Tester, in the nation’s most pivotal Senate contest next year and **[needs our help to win.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=39f7a6b507424c0aa64009c880647dba&_e=Tp2bKqK9SP-cvbSus1H7E9EAUivJHcEmKmZpgNxnNV1kzTWN4I2PNgDT7oebX7HDY2-_BxG-gCjoAUhNRSSmMvrgs_DL-Y3ffvnBKpwvAzwretHYbgmjNdym8Rvin_c0gNqv--nphOli_h_SYVgobwBTViiirxD-xXHAQuKc_DPnZFmoNfADNJjCyKVzYZ5BfFqrAYoal0BEA1gGY0_RGoCxuEbdR2MT3OGKSD0bdb24WGILNz2UJbluXvm8hmItAJqVGbvoOUcMaNMyM_4Mo4wfdujmNv0zV5AwaH6t4Uhu-oBWTyAcOVNRAwUwylW_5KKwxcZ6g0H4JL0LMTktAfgIvnI0OkmCnBZyWeF7Lq1B3scNMfPGThfu8u8k87P__Y-GC1KQpVBU8bD6SsEPkA%3D%3D)**

name: Hilary Franz
subject: I won’t sugarcoat it
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Hilary Franz (D)
body:
Look, I have been on the front lines for seven years. I won’t sugarcoat the reality: climate change is here.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66640517/417047779/-165497492?refcode=BLU-HFFG-20230717-1-EM&amount=25)
---
| Peter,

As Commissioner of Public Lands, I have been on the frontline of forest fire response and fighting climate change for seven years. **I am proud that Washington’s wildfire response has transformed under my leadership.** We don’t just respond to wildfires. We work to prevent wildfires.

**Unfortunately, even with the best wildfire response infrastructure we have ever had, climate change is making wildfire management harder than ever.** This past week, fires in central Washington forced families to evacuate their homes. Even worse, wildfire season is becoming more intense, with fire expanding in the earliest parts of the season.
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66640518/417047780/1028795134?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjFjODZhYzItZjEyNC1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=n02XPZPULAjyx1m5KT24mpyyPRc6ec1YJfU0e2eK4hU=&emci=5bc86ac2-f124-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&emdi=61c86ac2-f124-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&ceid=11003312)
_**I won’t sugarcoat the reality: climate change is here.**_ Climate change will affect everyone in the coming years but disproportionately affect vulnerable populations. Washington needs a comprehensive plan to save lives and preserve the land we call home. More importantly, we need to put the plan into action with urgency and efficiency.

Unlike my opponents, I have experience executing massive climate disaster responses and making difficult decisions when the stakes are high. I know I am the best candidate to lead Washington in this time of climate crisis.

As we say in firefighting: all lands, all hands, all together –

Hilary

clean_body: Look, I have been on the front lines for seven years. I won’t sugarcoat the reality: climate change is here. | | Peter, As Commissioner of Public Lands, I have been on the frontline of forest fire response and fighting climate change for seven years. I am proud that Washington’s wildfire response has transformed under my leadership. We don’t just respond to wildfires. We work to prevent wildfires. Unfortunately, even with the best wildfire response infrastructure we have ever had, climate change is making wildfire management harder than ever. This past week, fires in central Washington forced families to evacuate their homes. Even worse, wildfire season is becoming more intense, with fire expanding in the earliest parts of the season. I won’t sugarcoat the reality: climate change is here. Climate change will affect everyone in the coming years but disproportionately affect vulnerable populations. Washington needs a comprehensive plan to save lives and preserve the land we call home. More importantly, we need to put the plan into action with urgency and efficiency. Unlike my opponents, I have experience executing massive climate disaster responses and making difficult decisions when the stakes are high. I know I am the best candidate to lead Washington in this time of climate crisis. As we say in firefighting: all lands, all hands, all together – Hilary | = * | | Paid for by Friends of Hilary Franz (D) Friends of Hilary Franz (D) PO Box 9100 Seattle, WA 98109 United States | = Email is a critical way for our campaign to stay in touch with you. body:
**BREAKING: NEW GOP OPPONENT ENTERS OHIO SENATE RACE AGAINST SHERROD BROWN**
Sherrod needs your grassroots support now more than ever!
**[Donate Now ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/casey-em-20230717-1?amount=5&refcode=em20230717-1&refcode2=316_552475_WIhKS6&akid=316%2E552475%2EWIhKS6)**
* * *
**This news couldn’t have come at a worse time, Peter:**
After _The Cook Political Report_ called our race in Ohio a toss up, Mitch McConnell started coming after Sherrod as his top target for defeat. Now, a brand-new GOP opponent just jumped in the race to defeat Sherrod — and polls already show us in a dead heat.
With Senate control on the line, we need an immediate surge of grassroots support to pull ahead and win. That’s why we just set a $30k rapid-response goal to make sure we have the resources to defend this must-win seat, and we need your help to get there by midnight:

Your contribution doesn't just influence events -- it changes the fabric of this nation -- there's nothing more important than your support right now. We need to be ready for 2024, and with your help, conservatives will have the momentum and the war chests to beat back the radical left and TAKE BACK this nation. | Thank you for your support. Sarah (GOP Alerts) Paid for by Miller-Meeks for Congress Miller-Meeks for Congress P.O. Box 33 Ottumwa, IA 52501 If you would like to send donation by mail,

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Brian Williams for Congress
body:
| | Peter,

As Dr. Williams’ campaign manager, I wanted to reach out and personally thank you for stepping up for our campaign over the past two months. Having such a strong network of grassroots supporters has made all the difference in these early days!

This is an open seat, Peter, and we’ve already seen other candidates start to jump into this race.**But we know that Dr. Williams’ experience as an Air Force Veteran, trauma surgeon, and gun safety advocate makes him the best choice for TX-32.**

I am so honored to be part of this campaign. Dr. Williams has the true heart of a public servant and we’re building a campaign to make him the first trauma surgeon to ever serve in Congress — a vital voice that has been missing in Washington, that can lead to real change to address gun violence and much more.

[As we enter the new quarter, will you help us keep our momentum going and ensure we have the resources we need in this race by contributing $10, $25, or whatever you can today? ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66641411/417050828/-609881289?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_BW_20230717_FR_JulyMMFR_0_3MO_BerkeleyMashburn_na_na_MMFR02&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDU5MjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTUzOWM5ZGYtZjUyNC1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=w7NbITMWZ5Ppq4JVJ_3SWsnYQtiFu9Tp2GdmZgZMs_8=) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.
